subject: Mathematics
title: Poetry and Meter
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on Poetry and Meter.

hook.text: Shakespeare wrote in iambic pentameter: da-DUM da-DUM da-DUM da-DUM da-DUM. That's a pattern.

buildup.text: Poetic meter assigns stressed and unstressed syllables in repeating groups called feet.

discovery.text: Ancient Indian poet Pingala analyzed meter using binary-like notation—and found Fibonacci numbers in the process.

twist.text: The number of rhythms with n beats matches the Fibonacci sequence. Poetry and math share a heartbeat.

climax.text: From sonnets to rap, mathematical rhythm underlies every form of verse ever composed.

punchline.text: Every poem has a pulse, and that pulse is math.

quiz.question: What mathematical sequence appears in the analysis of poetic meter?
